title: for_each_clause correctly isolates regex state per if/elsif clause
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT AND SHOULD BE DEFENDED: validate_stmt uses for_each_clause to split a combined if/elsif stmt into separate clause slices before calling validate_regex_in_clause. This means the :regex tag and pattern in one clause cannot bleed into an adjacent elsif clause that happens to have its Str/StringList appearing later in the flat form list. The test compile_regex_invalid_in_if_clause_with_elsif covers this exact regression. The design is correct: Block boundaries are natural clause separators, and rposition within a clause slice (not the full stmt) correctly identifies the last pattern argument.
